
Notes on how to update DXMT to a newer version
-------------------------------------------------

We keep track of the external sources here in /vendor/dxmt.  The 'current'
directory is the currently used version, where as the version subdirs
like '0.60' are tags of 'current' for the corresponding version.

For general information about vendor branches, see:
  http://svnbook.red-bean.com/en/1.8/svn.advanced.vendorbr.html


Updating the vendor branch
--------------------------

The goal here is to make vendor/dxmt/current an exact replica of the
current dxmt tarball.  We use diff to find files and directories that
are added and removed.  There are other ways.

Steps (y.y.y = previous version, x.x.x = new version):

    0. Make sure /vendor/dxmt is correctly tagged, tag if needed.

    1. Clone the repository in /tmp and switch to the release:
            git clone --recursive https://github.com/3Shain/dxmt.git dxmt-x.x.x
            cd dxmt-x.x.x
            git checkout tags/vx.x.x
            git submodule init
            git submodule update

    2. Create a diff between 'current' and the tarball:
           cd vendor/dxmt
           diff -ruN --binary current /tmp/dxmt-x.x.x > /tmp/y.y.y-x.x.x.diff

    3. Apply the patch to vendor/dxmt/current:
           svn patch /tmp/y.y.y-x.x.x.diff

    4. Check that the tarball and 'current' matches, copy over and add any
       missing files (typically binaries):
           cd current
           diff -r . /tmp/dxmt-x.x.x
           cd ..
           svn st current | grep -F "?"     # or 'less' instead of grep

    5. Copy /tmp/dxmt-x.x.x/ on top of current/ to ensure there are no line
       ending issues or such.

       Windows:
           xcopy /s \tmp\dxmt-x.x.x\* current\

       Unix:
           cp ...

    6. Redo step 4 just to be sure.

    7. Commit current:
           svn ci -m "vendor/dxmt: Updated to x.x.x. bugref:xxxx"
           svn st       # (check if we missed anything)

    8. Tag it:
           svn copy -m "vendor/dxmt: Tagging y.y.y. bugref:xxxx" \
               "^/vendor/dxmt/current" \
               "^/vendor/dxmt/x.x.x"

P.S. There is no real need to check out the tags unless you want to look at
     the actual sources or something.


Updating the code in the VBox tree
----------------------------------

Update the source in the VBox tree can be done in several ways.


Variant I (y.y.y = previous version, x.x.x = new version):

    1. Start with a fresh dxmt copy:
           svn copy -m "libs: Copied unmodified dxmt vx.x.x sources from vendor branch. bugref:xxxx" \
               "^/vendor/dxmt/current" \
               "^/trunk/src/libs/dxmt-x.x.x"
           svn up src/libs

    2. Apply our changes:
           cd src/libs/dxmt-x.x.x
           svn merge  "^/vendor/dxmt/y.y.y" "^/trunk/src/libs/dxmt-y.y.y" .

    3. Resolve merge conflicts and make it build.

    4. Commit:
           svn ci -m "dxmt-x.x.x: Applied and adjusted our dxmt changes to x.x.x.  bugref:xxxx"

    5. Modify src/Makefile.kmk and Config.kmk to enable using the new version.

    P.S. All the merging and building fixes does not need to be one commit.


Variant II (y.y.y = previous version, x.x.x = new version):

    1. Copy our current dxmt to the new location:
          svn copy -m "libs: Copied our dxmt vy.y.y in preparation for vx.x.x upgrade. bugref:xxxx" \
              "^/trunk/src/libs/dxmt-y.y.y" \
              "^/trunk/src/libs/dxmt-x.x.x"

    2. Apply the upstream dxmt-native changes:
          cd src/libs/dxmt-x.x.x
          svn merge "^/vendor/dxmt/y.y.y" "^/vendor/dxmt/x.x.x" .

    3. Resolve merge conflicts and build it build.

    4. Commit:
           svn ci -m "dxmt-x.x.x: Applied and adjusted our dxmt changes to x.x.x.  bugref:xxxx"

    5. Modify src/Makefile.kmk and Config.kmk to enable using the new version.

    P.S. All the merging and building fixes does not need to be one commit.


The difference between the two variants is that the first one is likely to be
more gentle with subversion space.  The merge conflict resolving and such is
should be more or less the same.   OTOH, the second variant will better retain
our change history and is the one described in the svnbook link at the top of
this document.
